packages.name: block-buffer
packages.description: Buffer type for block processing of data

packages.name: cfg-if
packages.description:
A macro to ergonomically define an item depending on a large number of #[cfg]
parameters. Structured like an if-else chain, the first matching branch is the
item that gets emitted.

packages.name: chacha20
packages.description:
The ChaCha20 stream cipher (RFC 8439) implemented in pure Rust using traits
from the RustCrypto `cipher` crate, with optional architecture-specific
hardware acceleration (AVX2, SSE2). Additionally provides the ChaCha8, ChaCha12,
XChaCha20, XChaCha12 and XChaCha8 stream ciphers, and also optional
rand_core-compatible RNGs based on those ciphers.

packages.name: cipher
packages.description: Traits for describing block ciphers and stream ciphers

packages.name: convert_case
packages.description: Convert strings into any case

packages.name: convert_case
packages.description: Convert strings into any case

packages.name: cpufeatures
packages.description:
Lightweight runtime CPU feature detection for aarch64, loongarch64, and x86/x86_64 targets, 
with no_std support and support for mobile targets including Android and iOS

packages.name: crypto-common
packages.description: Common cryptographic traits

packages.name: curve25519-dalek
packages.description: A pure-Rust implementation of group operations on ristretto255 and Curve25519

packages.name: curve25519-dalek-derive
packages.description: curve25519-dalek Derives

packages.name: derive_more
packages.description: Adds #[derive(x)] macros for more traits

packages.name: derive_more
packages.description: Adds #[derive(x)] macros for more traits

packages.description: Internal implementation of `derive_more` crate

packages.name: digest
packages.description: Traits for cryptographic hash functions and message authentication codes

packages.name: fiat-crypto
packages.description: Fiat-crypto generated Rust

packages.name: futures
packages.description:
An implementation of futures and streams featuring zero allocations,
composability, and iterator-like interfaces.

packages.name: futures-channel
packages.description: Channels for asynchronous communication using futures-rs.

packages.name: futures-core
packages.description: The core traits and types in for the `futures` library.

packages.name: futures-executor
packages.description: Executors for asynchronous tasks based on the futures-rs library.

packages.name: futures-io
packages.description: The `AsyncRead`, `AsyncWrite`, `AsyncSeek`, and `AsyncBufRead` traits for the futures-rs library.

packages.name: futures-macro
packages.description: The futures-rs procedural macro implementations.

packages.name: futures-sink
packages.description: The asynchronous `Sink` trait for the futures-rs library.

packages.name: futures-task
packages.description: Tools for working with tasks.

packages.name: futures-util
packages.description: Common utilities and extension traits for the futures-rs library.

packages.name: generic-array
packages.description: Generic types implementing functionality of arrays

packages.name: getrandom
packages.description: A small cross-platform library for retrieving random data from system source

packages.name: getrandom
packages.description: A small cross-platform library for retrieving random data from system source

packages.name: hex
packages.description: Encoding and decoding data into/from hexadecimal representation.

packages.name: hex-literal
packages.description: A macro for converting hexadecimal string literals to a byte array at compile time

packages.name: hmac
packages.description: Generic implementation of Hash-based Message Authentication Code (HMAC)

packages.name: inout
packages.description: Custom reference types for code generic over in-place and buffer-to-buffer modes of operation.

packages.name: libc
packages.description: Raw FFI bindings to platform libraries like libc.

packages.name: memchr
packages.description:
Provides extremely fast (uses SIMD on x86_64, aarch64 and wasm32) routines for
1, 2 or 3 byte search and single substring search.

packages.name: nom
packages.description: A byte-oriented, zero-copy, parser combinators library

packages.description: Single assignment cells and lazy values.

packages.description: A lightweight version of pin-project written with declarative macros.

packages.name: ppv-lite86
packages.description: Cross-platform cryptography-oriented low-level SIMD library.

packages.name: proc-macro2
packages.description: A substitute implementation of the compiler's `proc_macro` API to decouple token-based libraries from the procedural macro use case.

packages.name: proton-vpn-yaourt
packages.description: Yet Another Obfuscation Utility for Resilient Transport

packages.name: quote
packages.description: Quasi-quoting macro quote!(...)

packages.name: r-efi

packages.description: Core random number generator traits and tools for implementation.

packages.name: sha2

Pure Rust implementation of the SHA-2 hash function family
including SHA-224, SHA-256, SHA-384, and SHA-512.

packages.name: slab
packages.description: Pre-allocated storage for a uniform data type

packages.name: subtle
packages.description: Pure-Rust traits and utilities for constant-time cryptographic implementations.

packages.name: syn
packages.description: Parser for Rust source code

packages.name: thiserror
packages.description: derive(Error)

packages.name: thiserror-impl
packages.description: Implementation detail of the `thiserror` crate

packages.name: tracing
packages.description: Application-level tracing for Rust.

packages.name: tracing-attributes
packages.description: Procedural macro attributes for automatically instrumenting functions.

packages.name: tracing-core
packages.description: Core primitives for application-level tracing.

packages.name: typenum
packages.description:
Typenum is a Rust library for type-level numbers evaluated at
    compile time. It currently supports bits, unsigned integers, and signed
    integers. It also provides a type-level array of type-level numbers, but its
    implementation is incomplete.

packages.name: unicode-ident
packages.description: Determine whether characters have the XID_Start or XID_Continue properties according to Unicode Standard Annex #31

packages.name: unicode-segmentation
packages.description:
This crate provides Grapheme Cluster, Word and Sentence boundaries
according to Unicode Standard Annex #29 rules.

packages.name: unicode-xid
packages.description:
Determine whether characters have the XID_Start
or XID_Continue properties according to
Unicode Standard Annex #31.

packages.name: version_check
packages.description: Tiny crate to check the version of the installed/running rustc.

packages.name: wasi
packages.license: Apache-2.0 WITH LLVM-exception OR Apache-2.0 OR MIT
packages.description: Experimental WASI API bindings for Rust

packages.name: wasip2
packages.license: Apache-2.0 WITH LLVM-exception OR Apache-2.0 OR MIT
packages.description: WASIp2 API bindings for Rust

packages.name: wit-bindgen
packages.license: Apache-2.0 WITH LLVM-exception OR Apache-2.0 OR MIT
packages.description:
Rust bindings generator and runtime support for WIT and the component model.
Used when compiling Rust programs to the component model.

packages.name: x25519-dalek
packages.description: X25519 elliptic curve Diffie-Hellman key exchange in pure-Rust, using curve25519-dalek.

packages.name: zerocopy
packages.description: Zerocopy makes zero-cost memory manipulation effortless. We write "unsafe" so you don't have to.

packages.name: zerocopy-derive
packages.description: Custom derive for traits from the zerocopy crate

packages.name: zeroize
packages.description:
Securely clear secrets from memory with a simple trait built on
stable Rust primitives which guarantee memory is zeroed using an
operation will not be 'optimized away' by the compiler.
Uses a portable pure Rust implementation that works everywhere,
even WASM!

packages.name: zeroize_derive
packages.description: Custom derive support for zeroize
